task: Unit tests for src/lib/concerts.ts with 80% code coverage
summary: Security assessment for comprehensive unit test suite covering concert CRUD operations, pagination, statistics, and spending aggregation. Focus on authorization/ownership verification, PII handling in test fixtures, and database query safety.

stride_analysis.scenario: Test fixtures contain hardcoded user IDs that could be mistaken for production user IDs
stride_analysis.mitigation: Use clearly identifiable test UUIDs (test-user-{N}-{uuid}) in all fixtures. Verify via code review that no production data is embedded.

stride_analysis.scenario: Tests for updateConcert/deleteConcert do not verify ownership checks; tests could pass even if authorization logic is removed
stride_analysis.mitigation: Write explicit NEGATIVE test cases: test_updateConcert_different_user_returns_null, test_deleteConcert_different_user_returns_null. Verify userId parameter is enforced in every mutation test.

stride_analysis.scenario: Test logs or coverage reports expose PII (user emails, concert locations, costs) from test fixtures
stride_analysis.mitigation: Use anonymized test data only (test-user-1@test.example.com, not real emails). Configure Vitest with minimal logging. Exclude test data from coverage reports.

stride_analysis.scenario: Test coverage for getConcertsPaginated does not verify that public profile filtering (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic) is applied
stride_analysis.mitigation: Write test cases that verify private user data is NOT returned: test_getConcertsPaginated_public_filter_hides_location, test_getConcertsPaginated_public_filter_hides_cost

stride_analysis.scenario: Tests for parsing functions (parseSupportingActIds) do not verify bounds checking; malformed input could cause stack overflow or excessive memory use

attack_surface_changes: New Prisma mock layer — verify mocks return correct data shapes (Concert, UserConcert, Band)
Test fixtures with pre-seeded concert/user/band data — ensure only anonymized test data is used
Cursor-based pagination tests — verify cursor validation prevents ID injection
Statistics caching tests — verify unstable_cache is correctly mocked

owasp_findings.finding: updateConcert and deleteConcert take userId parameter; tests MUST verify that non-owning users cannot modify/delete other users' concerts
owasp_findings.fix: Add negative test cases: test_updateConcert_non_owner_returns_null, test_deleteConcert_non_owner_returns_null. Verify these tests FAIL if userId check is removed.

owasp_findings.category: API3 - Broken Object Property Level Auth
owasp_findings.finding: getConcertsPaginated receives ConcertFilters with isPublic flag; tests must verify that hideLocationPublic/hideCostPublic are enforced at query level
owasp_findings.fix: Write tests verifying that when isPublic=true, concert.latitude/longitude are null and cost is null in response. Test that API route (app/api/concerts/route.ts) correctly applies these filters before returning data.

owasp_findings.finding: parseSupportingActIds function deserializes JSON from UserConcert.supportingActIds without size bounds; could be exploited if malformed data stored
owasp_findings.fix: Add test: test_parseSupportingActIds_max_100_items_rejected. Recommend: Add MAX_SUPPORTING_ACTS constant (e.g. 50) and reject arrays exceeding it.

owasp_findings.finding: getConcertsPaginated accepts limit parameter (clamped to 1-100). Tests should verify clamping is effective.

dependency_audit.cve_check: Vitest maintained by ViteJS maintainers. Check latest version for CVEs.
Stable, widely-used testing library. Verify version >= 14.0.0

auth_review.authentication_notes: concerts.ts is a server-side library; authentication happens at API route level (app/api/concerts/route.ts). Tests do NOT need to replicate auth, but MUST mock Prisma correctly.
auth_review.authorisation_notes: Ownership checks via userId parameter in updateConcert, deleteConcert. Public profile filtering via isPublic flag in getConcertsPaginated. Tests must verify these checks cannot be bypassed.
gaps: Tests must verify ownership checks (updateConcert/deleteConcert require userId match)
Tests must verify public profile filtering (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic applied when isPublic=true)
Tests for getUserConcerts must verify only the requesting user's concerts are returned (userId filter enforced)
auth_review.jwt_validation_notes: Not applicable — Better Auth uses session cookies, not JWT. Tests should verify Prisma queries filter by userId correctly.
auth_review.brute_force_needed_on: Not applicable — tests are not for API endpoints. Brute force protection should be tested at API route level (app/api/concerts/route.ts).

security_tests.scenario: User A attempts to update a concert created by User B via updateConcert(id, userA, input)
security_tests.implementation_note: Mock Prisma to return null for userConcert.findUnique when userId does not match. Verify updateConcert returns null, not throwing error or updating data.

security_tests.scenario: User A attempts to delete a concert attended by User B via deleteConcert(id, userA)
security_tests.implementation_note: Mock Prisma to return null for userConcert.findUnique. Verify deleteUserConcert is never called.

security_tests.scenario: Query concerts for a public user with hideLocationPublic=true, verify latitude/longitude are null in response
security_tests.expected: Returned concerts have city.lat=null, city.lon=null (location redacted)
security_tests.maps_to_owasp: API3 - Broken Object Property Level Auth
security_tests.implementation_note: This test likely resides at API route level (app/api/concerts/route.ts), NOT in concerts.ts tests. But document in TESTING.md that concerts.ts tests assume filtering is applied by caller.

security_tests.scenario: getUserConcerts(userA) called with Prisma mocked to return concerts for multiple users
security_tests.expected: Only concerts linked to userA via UserConcert are returned
security_tests.implementation_note: Verify Prisma query uses where: { userId } filter correctly.

security_tests.scenario: Create concert that matches existing concert (date, location, headliner within tolerance)
security_tests.implementation_note: Verify duplicate detection prevents data anomalies. Test COORD_TOLERANCE boundary (0.001 degrees = ~100m).

security_tests.scenario: parseSupportingActIds receives array with missing bandId, non-numeric sortOrder, etc.
security_tests.implementation_note: Verify the function validates each item's shape before adding to output. Test with circular references, null values, etc.

security_tests.scenario: getConcertsPaginated called with malformed cursor (not a valid UUID, SQL injection attempt)
security_tests.expected: Prisma parameter binding prevents injection; returns empty array or 0 results
security_tests.implementation_note: Test cursor boundaries (empty string, null, oversized string). Verify Prisma handles gracefully.

requirements: All test fixtures MUST use anonymized data (test-user-1@test.example.com, NOT real emails)
Concert fixtures MUST use well-known coordinates (Berlin: 52.52, 13.405) or anonymized cities
Band fixtures MUST use generic names (Test Artist One, Test Artist Two) without real band data
Session tokens in tests MUST be mock values (test-token-1), never real tokens
All image URLs MUST be null (never reference real photos)
UserConcert.cost MUST use test values (19.99, 0, null) not real prices from production
prohibited: NO production user IDs or emails in test fixtures
NO real concert locations that could identify individuals (e.g., home venues)
NO real API keys or credentials in test code or test data
NO PII in log output or coverage reports
test_data_gdpr_compliance.verification: Code review must check: grep -r 'gmail.com\|@company.com\|prod' __tests__/ — should find only test domain emails

security_test_coverage_gaps.gap: No explicit tests for ownership validation in updateConcert/deleteConcert
security_test_coverage_gaps.risk: Authorization bypass if userId parameter is ignored

security_test_coverage_gaps.gap: No tests for the fork/merge logic in updateConcert when concert has multiple attendees
security_test_coverage_gaps.risk: Data inconsistency if fork logic fails (user A's update unintentionally affects user B)
Duplicate concerts if coordinate matching fails or is too loose

security_test_coverage_gaps.gap: No explicit tests verifying statistics caching (unstable_cache) is correctly mocked
security_test_coverage_gaps.risk: Tests could pass locally but fail in CI if caching behavior is not replicated

security_test_coverage_gaps.gap: No boundary tests for getConcertsPaginated limit parameter (clamped to 1-100)
security_test_coverage_gaps.risk: Allows unrestricted resource consumption if limit clamping is removed

changes_required.description: Comprehensive test suite covering 25+ exported functions from src/lib/concerts.ts. Must include ownership validation, public profile filtering, cursor pagination, statistics caching, and edge cases.
security_focus: Cursor validation tests (injection attempts, malformed input)

changes_required.description: Add section: 'GDPR Compliance in Test Data' with guidelines for anonymized fixtures, prohibition on real PII, privacy flag testing, and test data validation checklist.
security_focus: Document test data anonymization rules (test@example.com pattern)
Add examples of hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic, isPublic flag tests
Clarify that tests should NOT reference real concert venues or user profiles

changes_required.description: Ensure Prisma is mocked globally and no real database queries execute during test runs. Verify vi.stubEnv() for feature flags.
security_focus: Mock prisma globally to prevent accidental database access
Ensure no credentials leak via console output

changes_required.description: Verify test scripts (test, test:watch, test:coverage) exist and are executable. Add pre-test validation to check for hardcoded secrets.
security_focus: yarn test must pass all tests
yarn test:coverage must generate coverage/ directory
CI should run: grep -r 'PASSWORD\|SECRET\|KEY' __tests__/ and fail if found

risks.description: Test fixtures contain real email addresses or user IDs from production → test data leakage in CI logs
risks.mitigation: Use only test@example.com domain in fixtures. Add pre-commit hook to validate test files do not contain @gmail.com, @company.com, or production UUIDs.

risks.description: Authorization checks (userId ownership) are not tested → updateConcert/deleteConcert can be modified to skip authorization without tests catching it
risks.mitigation: Write explicit NEGATIVE test cases that verify non-owners cannot modify/delete. These tests should FAIL if authorization is removed.

risks.description: Public profile filtering (hideLocationPublic, hideCostPublic) is not tested in concerts.ts → filtering could be bypassed at API route level without test coverage
risks.mitigation: Document in TESTING.md that concerts.ts library functions assume caller applies filtering. Add integration tests at API route level to verify filters are applied. Add JSDoc comments to concerts.ts functions noting their responsibilities.
risks.owasp_maps_to: API3 - Broken Object Property Level Auth, GDPR Compliance

risks.description: Prisma mock layer not configured correctly → tests pass locally but fail in production due to mocking differences
risks.mitigation: Use vi.mock('@/lib/prisma') globally in vitest.setup.ts. Create a realistic mock that returns correct data shapes. Add integration tests that use a test database (optional, for future PR).

risks.description: Statistics caching (unstable_cache) not mocked correctly → cache behavior differs between test and production
risks.mitigation: Mock next/cache in vitest.setup.ts. Verify unstable_cache is called with correct tags. Test that cache is invalidated after concert mutations.

risks.description: No bounds checking on parseSupportingActIds → malformed or oversized data could cause DoS
risks.mitigation: Add test for large arrays (e.g., 1000 items). Add MAX_SUPPORTING_ACTS constant to limit items. Test edge cases: circular references, deeply nested objects, null values.
risks.owasp_maps_to: A08 - Data Integrity Failures, API4 - Unrestricted Resource Consumption

recommendations.recommendation: Write explicit negative test cases for ownership validation. Structure tests to FAIL if authorization checks are removed. Example: test_updateConcert_non_owner_returns_null should FAIL if the userId check is commented out.
recommendations.rationale: Insurance data is sensitive. Authorization bypass could expose all users' concert data. Tests must be strong enough to catch accidental removal of security checks.

recommendations.recommendation: Enforce test data anonymization via pre-commit hook. Hook should grep for production domains, real UUIDs, and flag them as errors. Document in TESTING.md.
recommendations.rationale: Per GDPR requirements in MEMORY.md, test data must be anonymized. CI logs could leak real user data if tests use production email addresses.

recommendations.recommendation: Add JSDoc comments to getConcertsPaginated and related functions clarifying that the caller (API route) is responsible for applying hideLocationPublic/hideCostPublic/isPublic filters. Do NOT add filtering to concerts.ts — filtering should stay at API route level per existing design.
recommendations.rationale: Separating library logic from API concerns keeps code cleaner. But functions must document this contract. Tests should verify Prisma queries are correct; filtering verification belongs at API route tests.

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit test cases for cursor validation: empty cursor, null cursor, oversized cursor, cursor containing SQL injection attempts. Verify Prisma's parameter binding prevents injection.
recommendations.rationale: Cursor-based pagination is a common attack surface. Tests must verify that invalid cursors do not cause crashes or information disclosure.

recommendations.recommendation: Add comprehensive tests for updateConcert fork/merge logic when a concert has multiple attendees. Test scenarios: User A updates concert → fork created → User B still sees original → User A sees forked copy.
recommendations.rationale: Multi-attendee concert logic is complex. Data consistency bugs could cause users to see each other's private edits or lose data.

recommendations.recommendation: Test boundary conditions: concert dates at year/month boundaries, coordinate tolerance edge cases (0.0009 vs 0.001 degrees), limit parameter boundary (1, 20, 100), supporting acts array with 0 and max items.
recommendations.rationale: Edge cases are where bugs hide. Off-by-one errors in pagination or coordinate matching can silently fail.

mandatory_requirements: Authorization tests (ownership validation) are REQUIRED. Feature cannot merge without explicit test cases for updateConcert/deleteConcert non-owner scenarios.
Test data must be anonymized. No production emails, UUIDs, or PII in fixtures. Code review must verify.
Prisma mock must be configured globally in vitest.setup.ts. No real database queries during tests.
TESTING.md must be updated with GDPR compliance section (per MEMORY.md requirements).
All major functions (25+) must have at least one test case (not necessarily 80% line coverage on all, but 80% overall function coverage).
final_verdict: APPROVED_FOR_MERGE pending implementation of mandatory authorization and test data anonymization requirements. Security risk is LOW if recommendations are followed. CRITICAL if ownership checks or test data anonymization are skipped.
